passage.title: Library Return Cards
passage.text: Our group keeps a garden helper chart by the front desk. In the morning, Hana checks it before cleanup time. The garden helper chart has return slips for books and magazines for the day's job. Our teacher asks us to take the right note to the right room. Sometimes we compare today's slip with yesterday's slip and notice one small change. We like the garden helper chart because it helps us finish helper work on time.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the garden helper chart is used.

questions.prompt: Where is the garden helper chart?
questions.rationale: The first sentence says the garden helper chart is by the front desk.

questions.prompt: When does Hana use the garden helper chart?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Hana uses it in the morning.

questions.prompt: What does the garden helper chart have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the garden helper chart has return slips.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
